# Amajuba Economic Chamber of Commerce

> Promoting Growth & Prosperity across the Amajuba District, KwaZulu-Natal, South Africa.

## About

The Amajuba Economic Chamber of Commerce (AECC) is a registered organisation (Reg No: 2026/354235/08) that serves as a catalyst for sustainable economic development across the Amajuba District in KwaZulu-Natal, South Africa. The chamber convenes businesses, government, civil society, and academia to drive inclusive growth and community empowerment.

**Slogan:** Promoting Growth & Prosperity  
**Founded:** 2026  
**Registration:** 2026/354235/08  
**Region:** Amajuba District, KwaZulu-Natal, South Africa  
**Members:** 500+

## Mission

To serve as a structured platform that facilitates growth, innovation, and community empowerment — implementing targeted sectoral interventions across the Primary (Agriculture & Mining), Secondary (Manufacturing & Processing), and Tertiary (Services & Distribution) sectors, while building integrated competence across knowledge, skills, and attitudes for lasting economic transformation.

## Vision

To be the leading catalyst for sustainable economic development and inclusive prosperity in the Amajuba District — building a region where communities, businesses, and institutions thrive together.

## Core Value Proposition

Developing integrated competence — woven from knowledge, skills, and attitudes — to drive economic transformation and sustainable livelihoods across all sectors within 3–5 years.

## Economic Sectors

- **Primary Sector:** Agriculture and Mining — extraction and raw materials providing foundational resources.
- **Secondary Sector:** Manufacturing and Processing — Steel, Textiles, Chemicals, and Industrial processing.
- **Tertiary Sector:** Services, Distribution, Retail, Professional Services, and Logistics.

## Community Empowerment Programmes

- **Tendering & Procurement:** Training on municipal procurement processes, tender applications, compliance, and ethical participation.
- **Governance Literacy:** Building awareness of municipal structures, councillor roles, ward committees, civic rights, and public accountability.
- **Economic Participation:** Supporting cooperative development, informal trader growth, youth enterprise, and community-owned economic models.
- **LED Partnerships:** Collaborating with Newcastle Local Municipality and Amajuba District Municipality LED Units.

## Regional Footprint

Active across: Dannhauser, Utrecht, Osizweni, Madadeni, Newcastle, Charlestown.

## Contact
